2120822727.title: Market Research Analyst (Remote - within Europe)
2120822727.description:
About SlashData

SlashData helps product, platform, and engineering leaders understand how software development is changing in the AI era. For over 20 years, we have helped many of the world's leading technology companies understand developers, developer ecosystems, and the tools and workflows shaping the future of software.

Our work helps clients:

understand how the developer ecosystem and software engineering teams are evolving,
build better products, platforms, and go-to-market strategies for the AI era,
make smarter decisions about the tools, practices, and investments shaping the future of software

Our ambition is to become the industry-standard measurement system for the software development economy - the reference point that product, platform, and engineering leaders rely on to make decisions about developers, AI, and the future of software.

The Role

We are looking for a Market Research Analyst based within Europe with 2 to 5 years of experience in market research, data analysis, or technology advisory work.

In this role, you will develop and deliver research on how AI is reshaping software development - from developer tool adoption to engineering team workflows. You will work directly with product and platform leaders at major technology companies, translating our survey data into insights and foresight that shape their product, platform, and go-to-market decisions.

Prior experience in the software or technology industry and early experience in AI tools are major pluses, but deep curiosity about the space and a drive to develop genuine domain expertise are essential.

In this role, you will help turn business questions into research projects and research findings into clear, actionable insights. You will work closely with clients and internal stakeholders across survey design, analysis, reporting, and presentations.

You do not need prior experience in the software industry, but you should have a genuine interest in technology and a basic understanding of the software industry and developer ecosystem.

Responsibilities

Working with clients and internal stakeholders to translate business problems into research objectives and projects
Designing surveys that address the research needs of our clients and driving their desired outcomes (whether validating a thesis with data, or writing a thought leadership paper on the future of software). 
Analysing data (using Python) to extract actionable insights on developer behaviours, preferences, and market trends
Producing clear, well-structured reports that explain both the findings and their relevance (answering the "so what?" behind the results)
Presenting findings in client briefings, webinars, and other external-facing formats
Contributing to the design of our global developer surveys, asking the right questions given our research objectives
Actively building expertise in software development, AI, and the developer ecosystem - developing an informed point of view that shapes our research agenda and client conversations. 
Developing deep domain expertise in AI technology trends, developer tools, and software engineering practices - building a credible point of view, not just reporting data
Contributing to SlashData's published indices and trackers
Building and maintaining direct advisory relationships with client stakeholders, being a core part of the client relationship
Developing foresight on where AI technology trends are heading - helping clients see around the corner, not just understand what happened

Requirements

What we are looking for

Essential

2 to 5 years of experience in market research, data analysis, and client project delivery
Experience in working with stakeholders to understand business objectives and define research projects that address their needs
Excellent business writing skills (English), with the ability to effectively communicate complex findings to non-technical audiences
Demonstrated experience in writing clear, evidence-based research reports that turn data into actionable insights
Ability to identify the implications behind the data and answer the "so what?" clearly
Strong presentation skills, preferably with experience in delivering client briefings
Strong data analysis skills, including hands-on experience with Python, R, or similar scripting tools, and familiarity with SQL
At least a basic understanding of statistics and applied quantitative methods, such as segmentation, clustering and classification
Ability to visualise data clearly and effectively through easy-to-understand charts and graphics
Comfortable working as part of a distributed team, with mutual flexibility around working hours and occasional client meetings held after 17:00 UK time
Genuine curiosity about technology trends - you follow AI, developer tools, or software engineering developments because you find them interesting, not just because a job requires it
Confidence in client-facing settings - you can hold your own in a conversation with a Head of Product or VP of Engineering, not just present slides to them

Bonus points for

Experience in working with complex survey data
Advanced Python skills, including data manipulation and analysis in Pandas
Following trends in the tech industry, either through past experience or as a hobby
Having software development experience as a hobby or past work
Experience at an analyst firm, technology advisory firm, or in a developer relations/developer marketing role

You will be successful in the first 6 months if: 

Developed a credible point of view on at least one AI technology trend, informed by our survey data and your own research
Authored at least three research reports, carrying out all data analysis and background research as needed
Actively participated in at least two client research projects
Delivered at least two client briefings or webinars to our high standards
Actively contributed to shaping the research agenda and designing parts of the questionnaire of our upcoming global developer survey
Supported our developer outreach and sales teams, promptly responding to any requests relevant to your work
Built working relationships with at least 2 client accounts, actively participating in advisory conversations alongside senior analysts

Benefits

Benefits & What You'll Get

Meaningful Work at the Centre of AI & Software Evolution

Join a company shaping how technology leaders understand developers in the AI era. Your work directly influences platform strategy, tooling decisions, and the future of software engineering.

Competitive Compensation & Performance Upside

Competitive base salary depending on experience and location
Additional bonus based on company profitability

Truly Remote, Globally Connected

We are a fully distributed team across continents but we prioritise real connection.

Two team events per year:
One in-person international meetup (our last 4-day offsite was in Berlin)
One virtual global team event
Flexible working environment built on trust and ownership
No dress code

We combine autonomy with meaningful collaboration.

£1,000 Annual Personal Development Budget

We invest seriously in growth.

£1,000 per year training budget
Monthly Amazon book allowance (on any book you choose)
Exposure to senior leadership and strategic decision-making

We actively support continuous development, not just in theory.

Work Where You Work Best

£500 annual co-working budget to collaborate in person with teammates when working on projects
£500 initial home office setup budget (plus Laptop) to ensure you have the right equipment

You'll have the flexibility to choose the environment that maximises your productivity.

Lifestyle Perks

Spotify Premium or Netflix subscription
One self-care day to take whenever you need it

A Culture That Listens

We genuinely listen to our team.

Open access to leadership
Clear feedback loops
Real autonomy and accountability
Space to test ideas and improve systems

We're entrepreneurial, direct, and focused on impact, not hierarchy.

Salary

€35,000.00 - €45,000.00 (gross) depending on experience and location

mattrw@gmail.com.name: Matthew Kirby
mattrw@gmail.com.cv:
Matt Kirby CV (2026)

Matt Kirby
Bristol, UK
07495 802487
matthewrwkirby@gmail.com

Profile

Senior Research Analyst with 2+ years at NielsenIQ BASES, translating complex consumer and market data into commercial recommendations for global brands. Track record of influencing product strategy at senior director level, leading multi-market projects, and delivering insights that drive real decisions.

Experience

NielsenIQ BASES, Oxford, UK (Remote)
Senior Research Analyst (April 2025 – Current)
Research Analyst (Jan 2024 – April 2025)

Client Insight & Survey Development
Evaluated and reported on innovation potential and market viability using consumer survey data, presenting findings directly to senior managers, directors, and client teams.
Delivered data-backed launch recommendations that directly shaped client strategy, advising on activation strategies for brands like Cadbury and Philadelphia.
Designed survey questions and logical flows to address client briefs (e.g. assessing sensory impact of a recipe change for Philadelphia Original).
Analytics & Insight Methodology
Applied A/B testing, correlation analysis, and segmentation to uncover performance drivers and validate hypotheses about consumer behaviour.
Combined quantitative analysis with sentiment analysis and verbatim feedback for holistic recommendations.
Built dashboards in Studio (internal BI platform) and created visualisations for PowerPoint deliverables.
Maintained high data quality through QC and weighting processes.
Project Leadership & Team Development
Led end-to-end multi-market projects across up to 11 markets.
Managed timelines, stakeholders, and client communications.
Directed and reviewed work from offshore analyst teams (India).
Trained and onboarded junior colleagues and facilitated group training sessions.
Education

The University of Sussex, Brighton, UK
BSc Business & Management
September 2019 – May 2022

2:1 (68%) / 3.7 GPA equivalent
Dissertation included primary qualitative research and statistical analysis

Farnborough Sixth, Farnborough, UK
September 2016 – May 2018

A-Levels: Maths (A*), Economics (A), Biology (D)
Skills
Analytics: A/B testing, correlation analysis, segmentation, statistical significance testing
Data & BI: Excel (advanced), SQL (foundational), Power BI (in progress)
Reporting: PowerPoint, data visualisation
Leadership: Multi-market project management, stakeholder management, training & onboarding
